cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Leslie could have gone to the beach. First, I need to look at all the given information and see when Leslie was doing other activities. The key here is to find a time when Leslie wasn't doing anything else, right? Because if someone saw Leslie somewhere else, then they couldn't have been at the beach at the same time. Susan saw Leslie walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. So Leslie was there in the evening. Also, the beach was closed after 10pm, so Leslie couldn't have gone after that. So after class ends at 9am, they go to the movie. No gap between 5am and 11am. Then from 11am to 4pm at the bakery. Then from 4pm to 5pm at the store. The next activity is at 9pm to 10pm at the Statue of Liberty. So between 5pm and 9pm, there's no reported activity. Leslie was last seen at the electronics store from 4pm to 5pm. The activities are: Class: 5-9am Movie: 9am-11am Bakery: 11am-4pm Store: 4-5pm Then nothing until 9pm. So between 5pm and 9pm, Leslie is free. Also, the beach closes at 10pm, so going in the evening up until 10pm is possible, but since the latest activity is at 9pm, Leslie could have gone to the beach between 5pm and 9pm, then went to the Statue of Liberty at 9pm. So if Leslie was at the beach during that time, they couldn't be at both places. So C is not possible. D) 4pm to 5pm (Leslie was at the store then) So the only possible time is A) 5pm to 9pm.
